claims.claimText: Chronic Lateral Ankle Instability pathoanatomy centers on atfl, cfl, talus and related stabilizers.

metadata.claim_text: Chronic Lateral Ankle Instability pathoanatomy centers on atfl, cfl, talus and related stabilizers.

claims.claimText: Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating chronic lateral ankle instability.

metadata.claim_text: Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating chronic lateral ankle instability.

claims.claimText: Failed bracing and PT in functional athletes may lead to Brostrom lateral ligament repair discussion.

claims.claimText: State mechanism, key exam findings, imaging role, treatment concept, and return-to-play framework for chronic lateral ankle instability.

claims.claimText: Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for chronic lateral ankle instability — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.

metadata.claim_text: Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for chronic lateral ankle instability — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.

claims.claimText: Chronic lateral ankle instability follows inadequately rehabilitated inversion injuries with ATFL/CFL laxity.

claims.claimText: Rehabilitation milestones and return-to-play criteria for chronic lateral ankle instability require attending-defined progression gates.

decisionPoints.trigger: Active athlete with functional instability or mechanical symptoms from chronic lateral ankle instability despite appropriate initial management
decisionPoints.action: Discuss operative reconstruction versus continued rehabilitation; graft selection and fixation are attending decisions

decisionPoints.trigger: Athlete approaching return-to-sport after chronic lateral ankle instability treatment
decisionPoints.action: Apply sport-specific return-to-play criteria with strength, stability, and functional testing milestones
